author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Research complete. PyTorch BatchNorm uses 3 CUDA kernels:
1. batch_norm_collect_statistics: Welford online mean+variance per channel (one block per channel, warp shuffle reduction)
2. batch_norm_transform_input: elementwise normalize + affine per channel (one block per channel)
3. batch_norm_backward: per-channel gradient reduction + elementwise grad_input

Thread structure: blockIdx.x = channel index, threads grid-stride over batch×spatial.

For our PTX implementation, I'll combine statistics collection and transform into a single two-pass kernel for simplicity (pass 1: compute mean+var, pass 2: normalize+affine). This avoids the complexity of Welford's algorithm in PTX while being correct for the common case.
